name: Ahmad Vahidi
summary: Day 59 Sunday. CNN APRIL 24 LEAK: TRUMP ADMIN REVIEWING OPTIONS THAT INCLUDE TARGETING VAHIDI [REPORTED, single CNN source via unnamed officials]. The CNN leak — relayed by Defense News, Shabtab News, IBTimes Singapore — frames Vahidi as a 'central obstacle to reviving negotiations.' Plausible given operational logic (Vahidi is IRGC nominal commander, architect of Hormuz-interdiction posture) and historical precedent (Soleimani 2020 targeting was preceded by similar option-review leaks). Iran has NOT moved Vahidi to a hardened-shelter posture publicly; Iranian state media has not addressed the leak directly (Tasnim ran a Vahidi-positive 'Trump's strategic failure' framing per Pravda Trump April 24 relay). Open question: leaked TO PRESSURE Iran's negotiation track, or TO PREPARE PUBLIC for actual targeting? Both are operationally significant. PEZESHKIAN-VAHIDI-DEHGHAN-BLOCK STORY LIKELY BUSTED: Pezeshkian's April 22 Press TV IRGC-praising statement + Sunday April 26 phone call to Sharif (publicly hailed Munir's Tehran visit, restated 'imposed/forced negotiations' precondition) substantively contradicts the rupture framing. The Iran International seed-and-amplify cluster (8 Western outlets) has not produced new independent corroboration; Pezeshkian's continued public alignment with Vahidi-camp posture suggests the rupture story was probably one-side-framing-as-fact. Mojtaba Day 45 silence holds Sunday; Time April 21 'AI-generated videos' disclosure adds technical mechanism layer to the medical-incapacitation thesis. 'Vahidi-speaks-through-Mojtaba' architecture intact and now empirically supported (six-source convergence). Vahidi photograph at SNSC still absent.

current.status: operationally controlling state functions per multi-source REPORTED

signals.content: Axios (REPORTED, three US-officials) named Vahidi (IRGC C-in-C) and Zolghadr (SNSC Sec) as the war-continuation hardliners against the Ghalibaf+Pezeshkian+Araghchi engagement faction. US officials told Axios Vahidi 'practically controls the country, blocking Pezeshkian's intel-minister appointments.' First time US side has publicly named the IRGC factional architecture in this crisis.

Multi-outlet convergence on 'IRGC sidelines president, blocks appointments' framing: Fox News ('IRGC seizes control of Iran state functions, blocks president'), Euronews ('Iran's IRGC tightens grip on power as civilian leadership sidelined'), All Israel News ('Coup in Tehran? Hardline IRGC commander Vahidi reportedly seizes power'), Iran International ('Rift deepens between Iran's president and Guards chief'), American Almanac, Asatunews, Madhyamam Online, Quwa ('Pakistan's Mediation Crisis: Why the Real Power in Tehran Refuses to Come to the Table'). Specific procedural datum: Vahidi personally blocked Pezeshkian's attempt to appoint Hossein Dehghan as Intelligence Minister. Iranian administrative operation runs through 'a military council made up of senior IRGC officers that controls access to the centre of power.' France 24 carries a counter-framing piece ('Seriously fractured? Scepticism over Trump's Iran leadership split claim') arguing Trump may be overstating fractures for leverage. (Sources: Fox, Euronews, All Israel News, Iran International, American Almanac, Quwa, France 24)

Pezeshkian Wednesday online statement (operative Thursday) accused IRGC of undermining the ceasefire he and Araghchi negotiated through Pakistan mediation — implicitly naming Vahidi as the obstacle without using his name. House of Saud carried this under the framing 'Pezeshkian Cannot Fire the Generals Wrecking the Ceasefire.' For the first time in 56 days of crisis, Iran's civilian president has openly blamed Iran's military institution in a way that cannot be walked back. IRNA suppressed the accusation inside Iran; Press TV carried Araghchi's softer framing for international consumption — confirming Vahidi-camp control over Iran's state-media information channels in addition to its operational arm. (Sources: House of Saud, Iran International, Express Tribune via Pezeshkian's office direct push)

Wednesday IRGC kinetic against MSC Francesca (seized, anchored in Iranian waters per AIS) and Epaminondas (severely damaged but transiting back per Greek FM Gerapetritis) is now retrospectively read as Vahidi using operational facts to silence civilian dissent — a familiar pattern in Iranian state politics but unusual in its kinetic expression. CENTCOM Thursday announced 31 vessels redirected as part of the blockade — this scale of US blockade is the operational pressure Vahidi-camp is responding to. (Sources: AIS / Seatrade Maritime / Lloyd's List / CENTCOM via ABC News)

NYT SCOOP — MOJTABA 'HEAVILY RELIANT ON THE ADVICE AND GUIDANCE OF SENIOR IRGC COMMANDERS LED BY VAHIDI' [REPORTED single-scoop, David Sanger-desk sourcing]. ISW Critical Threats April 23 relay added the operational reading: Mojtaba's Friday 'fracture has occurred in the enemy' statement is a Vahidi-orchestrated message acknowledging the three-branch obeisance (Pezeshkian/Ghalibaf/Mohseni-Ejei) that itself is 'choreographed response shaped by both internal regime politics and external pressure from the United States.' This is the OPPOSITE-DIRECTION outcome the vahidi.json trigger-point file flagged: a Vahidi-orchestrated hardline signal rather than de-escalatory unifying intervention. Wednesday Pezeshkian-accuses-IRGC X post was NOT withdrawn; the unity messaging was layered on top rather than replacing it — the private factional architecture appears unchanged, while the public information operation has consolidated. No Friday IRGC kinetic — the MSC Francesca/Epaminondas day was Wednesday; kinetic pause during narrative consolidation. (Sources: NYT April 23, ISW Critical Threats Iran Update April 21-23, Press TV, Tasnim)
signals.verification: REPORTED (NYT single-scoop corroborated by ISW operational reading). Iranian state photograph of Vahidi at SNSC still absent — tag REMAINS at REPORTED.

signals.content: PEZESHKIAN-VAHIDI-DEHGHAN-BLOCK STORY AMPLIFIED ACROSS 8+ WESTERN OUTLETS — NO NEW INDEPENDENT CORROBORATION SATURDAY [REPORTED amplification]. Iran International origin reporting that Pezeshkian's intelligence-minister appointment attempts (including Hossein Dehghan) were blocked by Vahidi has been amplified Friday-Saturday across Fox News, Euronews, NaturalNews, AmericanAlmanac, Townhall, Charisma, TheDeepDive, AllIsraelNews, Trending Politics. Classic 'seed and amplify' disinformation pattern: one opposition-leaning Iranian primary becomes 'multiple Western reports' by repetition, not by independent corroboration. Substrate plausible per NYT-via-ISW April 23 + Critical Threats April 21 (Ghalibaf-Vahidi power struggle), but Saturday amplification has produced NO new independent source. Pezeshkian has not publicly reiterated Wednesday's accusation Saturday; office has not denied unity messaging. Iranian state media (IRNA, Press TV, Tasnim) have not addressed Vahidi-Dehghan claim — silence consistent with state suppression of intra-regime conflict. The structural reading remains: unity choreography is layered over an unresolved private rupture, exactly as ISW assessed. (Sources: Iran International origin; Fox, Euronews, NaturalNews, AmericanAlmanac, Townhall, AllIsraelNews, TheDeepDive relay cluster; ISW Critical Threats April 21-22)
signals.verification: REPORTED single-source origin + amplification cluster; no new independent corroboration.

signals.content: CNN APRIL 24 LEAK: TRUMP ADMIN REVIEWING OPTIONS INCLUDING TARGETING VAHIDI [REPORTED, single CNN source]. CNN reported the Trump administration is 'reviewing a range of military and strategic options that include the potential targeting of Ahmad Vahidi, the current commander-in-chief of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps,' framed as Vahidi being a 'central obstacle to reviving negotiations.' Relayed by Defense News April 24, Shabtab News, IBTimes Singapore. Plausible given (a) operational logic — Vahidi is the IRGC's nominal commander and architect of Hormuz-interdiction posture; (b) historical precedent — Soleimani 2020 assassination was preceded by similar option-review leaks. Iran has NOT moved Vahidi to a hardened-shelter posture publicly; Iranian state media has not addressed the leak directly. Tasnim ran a Vahidi-positive 'Trump's strategic failure' framing (per Pravda Trump April 24 relay), suggesting Iran reads the leak as pressure-leverage, not preparation. Open intelligence-grade question: leaked TO PRESSURE Iran's negotiation track (signal: deal or we kill your apex commander) or TO PREPARE PUBLIC for actual targeting? Both operationally significant.
signals.verification: REPORTED (single CNN source via unnamed officials, downstream relays).

signals.content: PEZESHKIAN-VAHIDI RUPTURE STORY LIKELY BUSTED — PEZESHKIAN'S APRIL 22 IRGC-PRAISE + SUNDAY MUNIR-PRAISE [REPORTED bust]. Pezeshkian's April 22 Press TV statement explicitly praised the IRGC ('IRGC thwarted enemies' plots, sinister goals'); Sunday April 26 45-minute phone call with PM Sharif publicly hailed Munir's Tehran visit and Iranian officials' 'constructive positions' in talks with the IRGC-architecture-aligned military leadership. Both substantively contradict the Iran International / 8-Western-outlet 'Pezeshkian openly accuses IRGC of wrecking ceasefire' framing. The seed-and-amplify cluster has produced no new independent corroboration through Sunday; Pezeshkian's continued public alignment with Vahidi-camp posture suggests the rupture story was probably one-side-framing-as-fact. The substrate of intra-regime tension remains plausible (NYT/ISW Vahidi-architecture framing intact), but the specific 'Pezeshkian publicly broke with Vahidi' claim no longer survives Pezeshkian's own subsequent statements.
signals.verification: REPORTED bust of seed-and-amplify pattern; Pezeshkian's own statements operationally contradict the rupture framing.

edges.context: Commander-in-Chief of the IRGC. Operational authority for fast-boat fire, ship seizures, Hormuz transit-certificate regime, $1/bbl toll booth.

Per multi-source REPORTED: Vahidi 'practically controls the country' through a military council made up of senior IRGC officers controlling access to the centre of power. Iranian state has not denied or confirmed.

Direct named adversary in Axios's factional map. Vahidi blocked Pezeshkian's appointment of Hossein Dehghan as Intelligence Minister. Pezeshkian Wednesday online statement implicitly named Vahidi (without using his name) as wrecker of the ceasefire.

Per NYT April 23: Mojtaba under frequent medical attention, 'heavily reliant on the advice and guidance of senior IRGC commanders led by Vahidi.' ISW reads Mojtaba's Friday 'fracture in the enemy' statement as Vahidi-orchestrated. 'Vahidi-speaks-through-Mojtaba' architecture operationally in place.
Vahidi as IRGC C-in-C has operational command over the IRGC Navy executing the Hormuz transit-certificate regime, $1/bbl toll, and commercial-vessel kinetic (MSC Francesca seizure 2026-04-22).

Day 59 Sunday. CNN April 24 leak: Trump admin reviewing options including targeting Vahidi. Either pressure-leverage (deal-or-we-strike) or preparation. Trump's 3-5 day window expired Sunday April 26 23:59 IST without unified proposal; Vahidi-as-target framing now structurally elevated. Vahidi's silence post-leak is consistent with discipline restraint OR genuine incapacitation/in-hiding.

Day 59 Sunday. Operational counter-party to the US blockade architecture; CNN April 24 leak adds direct-targeting dimension. CENTCOM 31-vessel blockade running rate ~1.3/day. Pentagon Hormuz-specific strike plans reportedly drafted (Türkiye Today April 24, REPORTED) target IRGC fast-boat infrastructure under Vahidi's operational command.

Day 59 Sunday. CNN Vahidi-targeting leak (REPORTED single CNN source, April 24) loads conditional defense-rotation. If leak is option-review pre-positioning rather than pressure-leverage, implies operational planning for Iran apex-target strikes — bullish for defense names with delay (equity prices 24-72 hours after operational confirmation). Combined with Pentagon Hormuz strike plans (Türkiye Today April 24, REPORTED) creates dual-leak setup that primes defense-rotation re-activation on confirmation.

trigger_points.condition: Tasnim or IRNA publishes photograph of Vahidi at SNSC, Mojtaba meeting, or substantive state-TV interview confirming 'in charge' role
trigger_points.mechanism: Iranian state has been silent on the Vahidi 'in charge' framing — silence is consistent with the claim but not confirmation. A Tasnim photograph at SNSC or a state-TV interview would upgrade the verification tag from REPORTED to CONFIRMED and convert 'coup in Tehran' from Western editorial to operational fact. Absence of such confirmation through end of Trump's 3-5 day window keeps the tag at REPORTED with continued caveat.

trigger_points.condition: Vahidi-orchestrated hardline statement issued in Mojtaba's name by Friday 23:59 IST 2026-04-24
trigger_points.mechanism: Mojtaba silence Day 42 trigger fires Friday 23:59 IST. If Vahidi-camp orchestrates a hardline statement in Mojtaba's name (or a Vahidi-ghostwritten message), the trigger fires but the downstream cascade is the OPPOSITE direction from the de-escalatory unifying intervention the original framework assumed. The fact that no Mojtaba-in-Vahidi's-name statement has been issued through Day 42 is itself consistent with Mojtaba being genuinely incapacitated rather than tactically silent. | RESOLVED 2026-04-24: TRIGGER FIRED in the OPPOSITE-DIRECTION flavour. Mojtaba's 'fracture has occurred in the enemy' statement published on X Thursday + Press TV/Tasnim/IRNA coordinated relay Friday. ISW reads as Vahidi-orchestrated; NYT April 23 scoop confirms Mojtaba 'heavily reliant on the advice and guidance of senior IRGC commanders led by Vahidi.' Downstream cascade is opposite-direction from de-escalatory unifier: hardline signal acknowledging three-branch obeisance. Content interpretation is the live question, not the silence.

trigger_points.condition: Pezeshkian publicly forces Vahidi out OR Vahidi forces Pezeshkian out within Trump's 3-5 day window (~April 26 IST)
trigger_points.mechanism: A unified Iranian proposal inside Trump's 3-5 day window requires Vahidi to override Pezeshkian OR Pezeshkian to override Vahidi. Vahidi has the kinetic; Pezeshkian has the international megaphone. Either direction would resolve the factional split and produce a proposal. Absence through April 26 = no proposal, Trump strike-list probability dominant. Falsifiable: 23:59 IST Sunday April 26. | RESOLVED 2026-04-26: TRIGGER NOT FIRED. Window expired without either side forcing the other out. Pezeshkian's April 22 IRGC-praising statement + Sunday April 26 phone call to Sharif (publicly hailed Munir-Tehran visit, restated 'imposed/forced negotiations') indicate factional consolidation by Pezeshkian aligning with Vahidi-camp posture, not by either removing the other. The Iran International seed-and-amplify Pezeshkian-Vahidi rupture story is likely BUSTED. Consolidation character is HARDLINE-REVERTED.

trigger_points.condition: Vahidi assumes hardened-shelter posture OR Iranian state media issues public denial of CNN targeting leak within 7 days (by 2026-05-03)
trigger_points.mechanism: CNN April 24 leak reports Trump admin reviewing options to target Vahidi. If Iran reads the leak as serious preparation rather than pressure-leverage, Vahidi would be moved to hardened-shelter posture (consistent with Soleimani-2020-precedent post-leak movement) OR Iranian state media would issue public denial/defiance. Neither has occurred Sunday — Iran reading the leak as pressure-leverage. Activation criterion: confirmed Vahidi-shelter movement OR Iranian state-media denial within 7 days. Absence of both confirms Iran-side pressure-leverage read. | ADDED 2026-04-26 at watching.
